title: US Iran War News LIVE: Jordan shoots down five Iranian missiles headed for Azraq
content:
Iran US War News LIVE: The United States has carried out multiple strikes in at least three regions of Iran after its Apache helicopter was allegedly downed in the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, June 9. Multiple explosions have been heard in Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Jask and Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz – a critical shipping lane. There has been no report of any casualties due to the US strikes on Iran. As America struck Iran at multiple locations, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has vowed to respond to the US strikes, saying Iran will leave “no attack or threat unanswered”. Track US Iran War News LIVE Updates only at Livemint.
After a number of explosions, the Iranian media now reports that the situation is now calm in southern part of the country. Justifying the strikes on Iran, the US Central Command said that the strikes were “a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression.”
Earlier, Trump had said in a post on Truth Social that it was Iran that had shot down the Apache helicopter and the US must, “of necessity, respond to the attack.”
The latest attack on Iranian territory after a series of strikes by Israel not only shatters the ceasefire, but also dents the ongoing peace deal negotiations between the US and Iran to end the war in West Asia. Trump had earlier said that the talks were making progress and that a concrete outcome could be reached within the next two to three days. Stay tuned for US Iran War News LIVE Updates.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held overnight calls with his Turkish and Saudi counterparts to discuss the fallout from US strikes on southern Iran. Araghchi condemned the strikes as a violation of Iranian sovereignty and territorial integrity, and said Tehran retained an inherent right to self-defence and a reciprocal response from its armed forces.
Jordan's military said on Wednesday it had intercepted and shot down five missiles launched from Iran towards Azraq. "The interception resulted in debris falling, but there were no casualties or material damage," the military said.
Global oil stockpiles in the world's 38 wealthiest nations have fallen to their lowest level since 2003, the US Energy Information Administration warned on Tuesday. With the Strait of Hormuz remaining largely closed, inventories are being drained at an accelerating pace, with global stocks expected to fall by 6.3 million barrels per day this quarter and a further 7.66 million in the third quarter.
As Middle Eastern crude stays sidelined, countries have turned to US supply to fill the gap. US crude and petroleum product net exports hit a record 5.8 million barrels per day in April, up sharply from an average of 1.4 million barrels per day last year.
Iran has called on Gulf nations to take responsibility for halting US and Israeli strikes, signalling that Tehran expects regional neighbours to intervene diplomatically or practically to stop the attacks.
Iran targeted several Gulf nations after US forces struck Iranian territory on Tuesday in retaliation for the downing of a US Army Apache helicopter. Jordan's military said it intercepted five Iranian missiles, while Bahrain sounded alarms and Kuwait activated its air defences to counter what it described as "hostile aerial targets."
US Central Command said the strikes were "a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression."
Nearly all Iranian missiles and drones targeting US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan were intercepted, according to initial US assessments cited by Reuters. US forces subsequently struck nearly 20 targets inside Iran, a US official told the news agency.
Iran US War News LIVE: The United States has struck nearly 20 targets in Iran, the US officials said.
They further report that it was not aware of any harm to US personnel or known damage to US locations after Iranian attack.
Iran US War News LIVE: As Iran launched targeted attacks in the Gulf – Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain – after the US strikes in south Iran, Jordan has said that it shot down five missiles that were launched from Iran towards Al-Azraq.
Meanwhile, the US officials have said that nearly all missiles and drones launched by Iran have been intercepted.
Iran US War News LIVE: The Indian share market is likely to open flat as tensions simmer in West Asia after renewed hostilities between the the United States and Iran, clouding hopes for a durable ceasefire and keeping global markets on edge.
Iran US War News LIVE: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday they had targeted four sites at the US al-Azraq base in Jordan using long-range missiles, Iranian media reported.
The Guards said the targets included F-35 fighter jet hangars and a command-and-control centre, and warned they were ready to deliver a "crushing and decisive" response to any further US attack.
Iran US War News LIVE: Kuwait army says air defences engaging 'hostile' aerial targets being launched from Iran.
Iran US War News LIVE: After targeting US bases in Jordan and Bahrain, Iran's revolutionary guards say they have targeted Ali Al Salem base in Kuwait with drones.
Iran US War News LIVE: Iranian media has said that the revolutionary guards have targeted a US base in Jordan with missiles.
Iran US War News LIVE: Bahrain's interior ministry has said that warning sirens have been sounded after Iranian attack and urged the public to heard to the nearest safe place.
Iran US War News LIVE: Iran has reportedly launched ballistic missiles towards US bases in the Gulf.
Mint couldn't independently verify the report.
Iran US War News LIVE: Explosions have been heard in Kuwait, Bahrain, Emirates, and northern Iraq as per local social media reports as Iran prepares to target the United States after strikes in southern Iran.
The American strikes come after an Apache helicopter was downed in the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran US War News LIVE: After hitting multiple targets in southern Iran, the US military now says that the US forces have completed their strikes against Iran.
Iran US War News LIVE: More explosions have been heard in Qeshm island in southern Iran as US continues its strikes.
Iran, in response, has targeted US assets in Bahrain.
Iran US War News LIVE: Iran's revolutionary guards have said that they have attacked US 5th fleet stationed in Bahrain with drones after the US struck regions in southern Iran earlier today.

Sebi Joseph, President of Otis India, talks about the company’s expansion plans, how the West Asia crisis is impacting the organisation, his leadership mantra, and more. He also talks at length on the order from real estate company Prestige Estates to install two super fast elevators that can travel at 10 metre/second at the under-construction The Prestige at Mahalaxmi, Mumbai which will be India’s tallest commercial building when complete.

title: Supreme Court declines urgent hearing on report over Saidulajab building collapse
content:
The Supreme Court on Monday (June 8, 2026) declined to accord an urgent hearing on a status report filed by a court-appointed amicus curiae seeking directions to the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) in connection with the collapse of an unauthorised five-storey building in Delhi’s Saidulajab area that claimed six lives and injured at least 14 others on May 30.
A Bench of Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and Atul S. Chandurkar declined to grant an urgent hearing on the report. “Mentioning rejected. No orders required to be passed,” the Bench said.
In his status report, senior advocate Ajit Kumar Sinha sought the Supreme Court’s intervention to direct the MCD to file an affidavit detailing how the illegal construction was allowed to continue and the action taken against officials responsible for the lapse. It also sought directions for a “structural audit” of unauthorised constructions across the national capital and for illegal structures to be sealed and demolished expeditiously.
The report was filed in proceedings arising out of a March 25 order passed by a Bench of Justices Ahsanuddin Amanullah and R. Mahadevan. While hearing a matter concerning illegal constructions in Tamil Nadu, the Bench had expressed serious concern over “widespread and blatant violations of building bye-laws and land-use regulations” and ordered a pan-India inquiry into the rampant conversion of residential premises for commercial use.
The Bench had appointed Mr. Sinha as amicus curiae to assist the court. In his report, he pointed out that the Bench had granted him liberty to seek the court’s intervention and appropriate directions as and when required.
‘Blind eye’
Mr. Sinha alleged that the MCD had failed to discharge its statutory obligations despite repeated notice of the violations. He submitted that the civic body had failed to seal the premises or take any action, even as additional floors were constructed illegally at the Saidulajab property.
The report filed through advocate Govind Jee stated that such unauthorised construction could not have continued for nearly a decade unless officials responsible for enforcing the law had “turned a blind eye” to the violations. It pointed out that MCD records themselves reflected a long history of unauthorised construction at the property.
According to the amicus, although the building was initially sanctioned only for a basement, ground floor and first floor, the illegal construction of the second and third floors was carried out in 2015. This was followed by the construction of the unauthorised fourth and fifth floors, resulting in the collapse of the entire building
“The gross violation and illegal construction which went on since 2015 ultimately led to the horrific and unfortunate incident where innocent students and residents of the locality lost their lives, whereas the beneficiary of the said illegal structures, as well as officers who failed to discharge their statutory obligations, are still enjoying the benefits derived from the unauthorised construction and by letting the illegality continue in perpetuity,” the report stated.
‘A mere eyewash’
Mr. Sinha contended that the suspension of two MCD engineers following the mishap was a “mere eyewash” and a “post-facto and cosmetic exercise” by the civic body.
Accordingly, the amicus urged the apex court to direct the MCD to file an affidavit detailing the action taken against illegal and unauthorised constructions in the national capital, as well as the unauthorised commercial use of residential premises. He also sought directions for a “structural audit” of such properties and for illegal structures to be sealed and demolished expeditiously.
Taking note of the fatalities caused by the mishap, the amicus also sought directions to the Delhi government to furnish an affidavit detailing the compensation proposed for the families of the deceased.
‘Fraud on the system’
In its March 25 order, the top court had directed municipal authorities to conduct surveys of illegal constructions and cautioned that the exercise should not become a “mere ritualistic” formality. It had also directed all statutory appellate authorities and quasi-judicial forums dealing with violations of building norms and unauthorised constructions to dispose of pending matters, as far as possible, within three months.
Notably, the court had also expressed strong reservations about the MCD’s stand on the issue of unauthorised change of land use. Recording its dissatisfaction with the civic body’s submission that Delhi’s Master Plan 2021 permitted multiple uses, the Bench headed by Justice Ahsanuddin Amanullah had observed that while the master plan may allow different categories of use, a person who obtains sanction for a building for a particular purpose cannot subsequently alter its use. Such a change, the court said, would amount to “nothing short of a fraud on the system”.
“This aspect appears to have been completely glossed over by the Municipal Corporation of Delhi and may even indicate tacit collusion and connivance with the violators for extraneous considerations, which are neither difficult to comprehend nor unknown,” the Bench had recorded in its order.
Published - June 09, 2026 01:03 am IST
